title: corynocarpus laevigatus
intro: Corynocarpus laevigatus, also known as the karaka tree, is a native evergreen tree to New Zealand, producing small, yellowish-green fruit that is edible for birds. The seeds and fruit of the karaka tree have been used in traditional Maori medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The seeds and leaves of the karaka tree are rich in antioxidants and have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the seeds and leaves of the karaka tree are often used to make teas, infusions, and capsules to harness their medicinal and health benefits.
